name: Gödel Completeness Theorem — Proof Graph
description: Semantic completeness of first-order logic: every sentence that is true in all models of a consistent theory is provable. The algorithm capsule is the Henkin witness-scheduling step that keeps consistency while closing existentials.
mermaid:
flowchart TD
  T0["Target: completeness of first-order logic"] --> G0["Given: deduction relation and semantic consequence for FOL"]
  T0 --> A0["Assumption: theory T is syntactically consistent"]
  A0 --> C0["Construction: enumerate formulas of the language of T"]
  C0 --> C1["Construction: adjoin Henkin witnesses for existential formulas"]
  C1 --> P0["Algorithm capsule: build witness-adjunction schedule preserving finite satisfiability"]
  P0 --> A1["Assertion: the Henkin precursor H remains consistent"]
  A1 --> C2["Construction: extend H to a complete maximal consistent theory T star"]
  C2 --> C3["Construction: form the Henkin term model M from closed terms modulo equality of T star"]
  C3 --> L0["Lemma: for sentences sli, T star proves sli iff M models sli"]
  L0 --> A2["Assertion: M models every formula proved by T star"]
  G0 --> S0["Given: soundness of the Hilbert-style or natural deduction calculus"]
  S0 --> A3["Assertion: theorems of T are true in every model of T"]
  A2 --> A4["Assertion: if T semantically entails phi then phi is in T star closing"]
  A3 --> A4
  A4 --> CO["Conclusion: every semantically valid consequence is formally provable from T"]
